lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Lithil
lines.text: Greetings, human.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Lithil
lines.text: I'm Lithil. Haven't you heard of me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can't say I have...

lines.speaker: Lithil
lines.text: ...Oh, well that's no good.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Should I? Did I miss some glorious feat of yours?

lines.speaker: Lithil
lines.text: You must run in all the wrong circles. It's fine, I won't hold it against you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're a demon! Usually I'm kill-

lines.speaker: Lithil
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing... You're right, wrong circles and all that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why are you here?

lines.speaker: Lithil
lines.text: Me? Can't a demon have a look around without all the questions?

lines.speaker: Lithil
lines.text: Alright! I heard there were other demons in Morytania, other than that oaf, Mazchna. I've come looking for them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not a fan of the slayer master?

lines.speaker: Lithil
lines.text: If he truly is as great as he says, he'd fight me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He didn't want to prove himself?

lines.speaker: Lithil
lines.text: Ugh, of course not. The fool says he won't fight a woman. That demon thinks he's a pillar of chivalry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That is a little suspicious.

lines.speaker: Lithil
lines.text: Yes. Anyway, to fully answer your initial question, I found demons here to the north and some lesser demons to the east.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are they doing here?

lines.speaker: Lithil
lines.text: Demons tend to stay where they were summoned. You see, once they're done eating the stupid wizard that thought it could control a demon, they're pretty much stuck until someone else decides to summon them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Summoning, aye? Sounds like a dangerous skill to practise.

lines.speaker: Lithil
lines.text: Be even warier of the imps. They'll steal the boots from your feet if you give 'em the chance.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't see how that's worse than being eaten, but thanks, I'll keep an eye out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Bye!

lines.speaker: Lithil
lines.text: See you 'round, human.
